name: Chip
description: Informational chip. Square-cornered metadata label. Passive (or dismissable via trailing ×). No leading icon. Background paints a translucent black/white overlay (light → `ref.palette.black.200`, dark → `ref.palette.white.200`) so the tag adopts whatever surface sits behind it without pinning to a fixed neutral step.

trailingIcon.description: Dismiss glyph (×); when present, chip becomes interactive.
Dismiss / opt-out glyph after the label.

default.label: sys.color.onSurface
default.note: Background is a translucent black/white overlay (`black.200` in light mode, `white.200` in dark) so the tag harmonises with whatever surface sits behind it — body, raised card, BottomSheet — instead of pinning to a fixed neutral step.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the chip is in. Applies only when the chip is interactive (a dismiss trailingIcon is present). The `states.focused` block above is kept for JSX runtime consumers; this block is the parallel external-reader contract.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: Action affordance with breathing room around it; the 3px outward extent is reserved by the surrounding layout.
focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)
